runs.selection_reason: Default 00 cadence selected Reporter. Today's ledger was missing and no queue pressure existed yet, so the highest-value action was daily state initialization and first report.
actions: Read required entrypoint, doctrine, loop, ledger template, package, catalog, manifest, and operations log files.
Confirmed today's status.json and queues.json were missing.
Initialized daily state and report without ingesting third-party content.

runs.selection_reason: Default 00 cadence still selected Reporter. Existing daily ledger and empty queues showed no concrete queue pressure to override the cadence, so the bounded action was a clean Reporter checkpoint rather than inventing discovery, review, or normalization work.
actions: Read required entrypoint, doctrine, loop, ledger, queue, report, package, and manifest context.
Confirmed today's queues remain empty across discovery, review, normalization, catalog, risk, packaging, publication, maintenance, and critic.
Recorded a no-ingestion Reporter checkpoint and preserved next action for Radar.

runs.selection_reason: Default 01 cadence selected Radar. The daily ledger had no queue pressure after two Reporter checkpoints and explicitly identified Radar as the next useful action, so discovery seeded concrete candidates for Source Reviewer rather than inventing normalization or catalog work.
runs.queue_item: created review queue items review-mcp-20260702, review-skill-security-20260702, review-agent-skills-standard-20260702, review-swe-skills-bench-20260702, review-agentskillos-20260702
actions: Searched current public web and GitHub for public agent skills, skill directories, agent loops, slash-command/runbook/runtime sources, MCP procedures, and benchmark/evaluation repositories.
Created five candidate source profiles with URL, provenance clues, license status, runtime targets, risk level, ingestion status, and next action.
Queued five Source Reviewer items and avoided normalization because license, security, and usefulness review are not complete.

runs.selection_reason: Default 04 cadence selected Cataloger, but catalog queue pressure was zero while five review items were open. Review and safety outrank catalog growth, so Source Reviewer consumed the highest-priority MCP review item.
actions: Read required entrypoint, doctrine, loop, ledger, queue, candidate source, and operations context.

Verified official MCP documentation and repository provenance, MIT license for the official specification/documentation repository, adoption signals, maintenance signals, and high-risk external-tool surfaces.
Created a reviewed reference-only source profile for MCP and queued Risk Auditor follow-up before any normalization or publication.

runs.selection_reason: Default 06 cadence selected Packager. Packaging queue was empty, but a bounded package-surface inspection was justified because installability and catalog coherence are Packager responsibilities.
actions: Read required entrypoint, doctrine, loop, ledger, queue, package, bin, manifest, catalog, report, and operations context.
Verified package name, version, license, CLI binary surface, npm files list, install root, install manifest, README install instruction, and review-flag install note.
Found install-facing catalog drift between dist/catalog.md and dist/catalog.json and queued Cataloger reconciliation instead of rebuilding artifacts from the Packager role.

runs.selection_reason: Default 09 cadence selected Normalizer, but normalization queue was empty while one high-priority MCP risk item was open. Review and safety outrank growth, so Risk Auditor consumed the MCP checklist item before normalization.
actions: Read required entrypoint, doctrine, loop, ledger, queue, reviewed MCP source profile, and operations log context.
Reviewed current public MCP security research and reporting for tool poisoning, prompt injection, STDIO/execution, cross-tool privacy disclosure, and multi-tool poisoning risks.
Created a durable MCP external-tool security checklist and updated the MCP source profile to reviewed-reference-only-risk-audited.
Removed the completed risk queue item and created Normalizer queue item normalize-mcp-external-tool-security-checklist-20260702.

runs.selection_reason: Default 12 cadence selected Publisher, but publication queue pressure was zero while a high-priority, risk-audited normalization item was open. Because publication must not endorse unreviewed or unsafe entries, Normalizer consumed the MCP checklist item first.
actions: Read required entrypoint, doctrine, loop, ledger, queue, MCP risk audit, reviewed MCP source profile, and operations log context.

Created skills/mcp-external-tool-security-review.md as a compact OKF-compatible local skill with trigger, inputs, procedure, verification, failure modes, attribution, high risk, and requires_review metadata.
Removed the completed normalization queue item and created catalog-mcp-security-skill-20260702 for Cataloger rebuild/reconciliation.

runs.selection_reason: Default 13 cadence selected Source Reviewer. Review queue still had three open source candidates and the highest-priority item was high-risk agent skill security research, so review and safety remained more concrete than catalog or publication work.
actions: Read required entrypoint, doctrine, loop, ledger, queue, candidate source, existing review plan, operations log, arXiv paper record, and public reproduction repository context.
Accepted agent skill security research as reviewed-reference-only because it directly informs defensive third-party skill review, but license and redistribution status remain unresolved.
Created reviewed source profile sources/reviewed/agent-skill-security-research.md without copying paper text, code, datasets, scanner prompts, payloads, or attack examples.
Removed the completed review queue item and created Risk Auditor queue item risk-third-party-skill-security-checklist-20260702.

blockers: requires_review_count was not available from the inspected catalog summary and should be computed by a future Cataloger pass.
Three public source candidates still require Source Reviewer inspection before catalog endorsement, package changes, or publication.
MCP now has a local high-risk, requires-review skill, but catalog/package/publication remain blocked until Cataloger rebuilds and reconciles catalog surfaces.
Reviewed agent skill security research remains blocked from normalization until Risk Auditor creates a defensive checklist and license/redistribution boundaries remain reference-only.
Install-facing catalog drift detected: dist/catalog.md reports 1173 skills and 2 high-risk entries while dist/catalog.json reports 1170 skills and 1 high-risk entry.
The new MCP security skill is not reflected in dist/catalog.json, dist/catalog.md, dist/install-manifest.json, or reports until Cataloger runs.
next_action: Risk Auditor should consume risk-third-party-skill-security-checklist-20260702. Cataloger should then consume catalog-mcp-security-skill-20260702 and catalog-reconcile-dist-catalog-surfaces-20260702 together to rebuild/reconcile catalog surfaces. Source Reviewer should later consume review-agent-skills-standard-20260702.
